sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund 12 months of Eternl operations, maintenance, and improvements, including frontend and backend maintenance and development, infrastructure, support, administration, and audits/oversight.

Deliverables: 12 months of operations from August 2026 to July 2027, Frontend maintenance and cross-platform development, Backend infrastructure and operations, User support and day-to-day operations, Governance tooling enhancements, Periodic independent audits of treasury fund use and oversight metrics, Conversion of up to $420,000 worth of Ada into stablecoins with any excess Ada returned to the treasury

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Eternl requests ₳2,350,000, valued at about $0.1787 per Ada, to cover around $420,000 in annual costs for a 12-month delivery period from August 2026 to July 2027.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposed budget allocates 55% to frontend, 25% to backend, 5% to support, 13% to administration, and 2% to audits.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal funds 6.0 FTE across 10 contributors at $70,000 per FTE.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Eternl plans to introduce Pro plans priced at $96 per year for personal users and $360 per year for company users, and says about 5,500 paying users would fully cover the annual $420,000 cost.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Eternl has about 100,000 browser-extension installs and about 30,000 Android and iOS installs.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal states that up to $420,000 worth of Ada will be converted into stablecoins and deposited into a public company wallet, with excess Ada returned to the treasury.
missing: Independent evidence for: Eternl has about 100,000 browser-extension installs and about 30,000 Android and iOS installs.
Independent evidence for: Eternl states that 10-18% of Cardano mainnet transactions are conducted via Eternl.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Eternl requests ₳2,350,000, valued at about $0.1787 per Ada, to cover around $420,000 in annual costs for a 12-month delivery period from August 2026 to July 2027." and clears the evidence gates.
Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Eternl has about 100,000 browser-extension installs and about 30,000 Android and iOS installs." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
